name: sovereign-pm
description: Sovereign PM web app (Node.js/React) — replaces this file-based PM system. Self-hosting the sprint tooling.
sprintGoal: Deliver the documentation layer for the Sovereign Platform — quickstart guide, architecture reference, and all four provider guides — so any developer can get from zero to running cluster in 15 minutes.

stories.title: Documentation: quickstart, architecture, and all provider guides
stories.description: Write docs/quickstart.md (15 minute path from zero to running cluster). Write docs/architecture.md (explains the bootstrap sequence, App-of-Apps pattern, namespace layout, security model, all services with their ingress URLs). Write docs/providers/aws-ec2-free-tier.md, docs/providers/digitalocean.md, docs/providers/vultr.md, docs/providers/bare-metal.md. Update README.md with badges, architecture diagram (ASCII), and contribution guide.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/quickstart.md covers: prerequisites, clone repo, fill config.yaml, run bootstrap.sh, verify
docs/architecture.md explains all phases and includes service URL table
All 4 provider docs exist with cost estimates and step-by-step instructions
README.md has ASCII architecture diagram showing service relationships
stories.testPlan: markdownlint docs/ README.md exits 0; verify each file listed in ACs exists with ls -la; grep -i 'cost' docs/providers/*.md to confirm cost estimates are present.

smart.notes: Specific 4: All target files are named but content expectations are loosely defined (e.g. 'covers prerequisites' is not a binary-checkable command without subjective reading). Measurable 4: AC 1 ('covers: prerequisites, clone repo...') requires subjective judgement on completeness; markdownlint and grep checks are solid but cannot verify adequacy of step-by-step content. Achievable 3: 7 documentation files including 4 distinct provider guides (each requiring cost estimates and step-by-step instructions) is near the top of a single Ralph iteration budget — borderline for 2 points. Relevant 3: Story matches the sprintGoal text but conflicts with the increment's declared theme ('sovereign-pm' web app, Node.js/React). Documentation could be deferred without blocking any implementation work; the mismatch between increment metadata and sprintGoal should be resolved. TimeBound 4: Description mentions 'badges' and 'contribution guide' for README.md that are absent from the acceptance criteria, creating minor scope creep risk.
